answer:
Yes — marginally. Your unit economics clear the bar: LTV = ($120 × 80%) / 3% = $3,200, so LTV/CAC = 4x (benchmark: >3x), and CAC payback = $800 / $96 = ~8.3 months (benchmark: <12–18 months).

The red flag is churn: 3% monthly ≈ 31% annualized logo churn, which investors will see as weak product-market fit or an SMB segment problem. Expect that to dominate the diligence conversation. Fix retention (or show strong expansion revenue / net revenue retention >100%) and the story becomes clearly fundable rather than borderline.
